title: Atylus fucifolia
taxonomy: Atylus fucifolia is a species of isopod, specifically a type of woodlouse. It belongs to the family Trachelipodidae, which is a group of terrestrial crustaceans. The genus Atylus is classified within the subfamily Trachelipodinae. Atylus fucifolia is a relatively rare species and not much information is available about it. Further research is needed to fully understand its classification and characteristics.
uses: Atylus fucifolia, also known as the beach morning glory, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help treat wounds and infections. Its extracts have also been used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. Additionally, the plant has been reported to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help protect against cell damage and infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Atylus fucifolia for medicinal uses.

parts: Atylus fucifolia, also known as the beach morning glory or coast morning glory, is a plant species native to Australia.  Atylus fucifolia has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous people of Australia for various purposes. The medicinal parts of Atylus fucifolia include the leaves and roots, which are believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties.
